title: machine: fix virtiofs mount targets on FCOS
body:
On Fedora CoreOS, /home is a symlink to var/home (same for /mnt, /opt, /root, /srv). When podman machine generates systemd mount units inside the FCOS VM via ignition, it uses the user-supplied target path directly as the `Where=` value. systemd refuses to start such a unit if the path is non-canonical:

```
home-alice.mount: Mount path /home/alice is not canonical (contains a symlink).
home-alice.mount: Failed with result 'resources'.
```

This breaks the default home directory mount on Linux hosts where `$HOME` expands to a path under `/home`. macOS is not affected since home dirs live under `/Users`.

Fix: add `canonicalizeFCOSMountTarget()` which rewrites any known FCOS symlink prefix to the real path before the ignition unit is written. The unit file name is updated to match.

Fixes: #28911

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Fixed `podman machine` failing to mount the home directory on Linux when the host home directory is under a path that FCOS symlinks (e.g. /home -> var/home).
```
